preamble: A variant mapping of nasal diphthongization where a nasalized low vowel develops an u-glide, common in Romance phonological evolution.

examples.note: Historical development where Latin endings like -anum, -anem, and -onem merged into the nasal diphthong -ão (mapped here as 'ou' for the diphthongal quality).

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
